Gold.data_anomaly: fast_info prev_close $4702.70 (-0.3%) vs history prev_close $4651.50 (+0.8%). Using fast_info as headline; history used for technicals only.

macro_summary: Markets closed fractionally higher on Friday with SPY +0.16% to $655.83 and QQQ +0.19% to $584.98, consolidating the Q1 relief rally with VIX easing a further 2.7% to 23.87. The dominant story remains the Iran-Hormuz oil shock, now in its 28th day — per Bloomberg, Trump renewed threats over the weekend to attack Iranian infrastructure if Hormuz remains closed, with oil surging and stock futures falling in Sunday night trading. The Fed's higher-for-longer stance remains intact with US10Y at 4.31%, and the Google TurboQuant narrative continues to cast a structural shadow over the semis cluster despite the tactical bounce.
commodities_note: WTI closed at $110.61 (-1.24% Friday) but Bloomberg reports Brent climbed 1.4% above $110/bbl in Sunday trading on Trump's escalation threats — the weekend move is the more relevant number heading into Monday's open. Per a Reuters-cited report, JPMorgan warns WTI could spike above $150 if Hormuz remains disrupted into mid-May. Gold shows a data anomaly: the fast-info source implies a -0.3% daily move from $4,702.70, while the historical data suggests a +0.8% move from $4,651.50; the fast-info figure of $4,687.80 is used as headline but the divergence should be treated with caution — use the 5-day trend rather than today's single-day change for gold positioning. DXY is essentially flat at 100.03 as the dollar holds its safe-haven premium. Given WTI's strength above $100 and the Hormuz crisis showing no signs of resolution, no attractive energy ETF entry exists — XLE is 7.1% above its 50-day average with the entry gate hard-blocked.

risk_reward.invalidation_reason: 50-day SMA -- break below confirms rally failure

D05_SI.signal_rationale: Signal changed from HOLD to WATCH — DBS has reclaimed its 50-day average (SGD 57.35) for the third consecutive session, closing at SGD 57.70 with RSI at 58.1, upgrading the technical posture from neutral to constructive. However, the R:R ratio is distorted (rr_distorted=true) with a tight stop just 0.6% below the 50-day average at SGD 57.35, making position sizing critical — invalidation at SGD 57.35 is within normal intraday volatility. Use SGD 57.00 (1.2% below, structural support) as the meaningful mental stop. Resistance sits at SGD 58.29, limiting near-term upside to ~1%. Volume is below average, which argues against a full position. The dividend yield of 5.32% at 1.1× book supports patience, but per RHB's March note, Singapore banks face 'modest operating environment' ahead with NIM compression ongoing as US10Y stays well above the 4.20% reset threshold. Analyst consensus expects an 8.8% earnings decline — a notable drag on the WATCH designation. Forward PE at 14× is in line with cluster median, offering no discount. Bottom line: DBS is fairly priced for the yield it offers, but NIM headwinds mean you're waiting for rates to turn before this becomes a full BUY. Actionable above SGD 58.29 with volume confirmation.

O39_SI.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at CAUTION. OCBC is 5.1% above its 50-day average at SGD 21.31, triggering a hard block on entry — the pipeline's block criteria are met and cannot be overridden. RSI at 67.9 adds to the extended picture, with the 9 days above the 50-day SMA well short of the 30-session sustained trend exception threshold. The derived R:R of 1.0:1 (to SGD 23.52 resistance vs. SGD 21.31 invalidation) is unattractive at current levels, and the rr_quality is 'derived' rather than observed. At 3.75% yield and forward PE 12.5× — a 10.7% discount to the Singapore cluster median — OCBC is the most attractively valued Singapore bank, and unlike DBS and UOB it showed YoY profit growth. But the thesis requires a pullback before entry. The structural support below at SGD 18.48 (200-day SMA, 17.5% below) is a wide air gap if the rally reverses. Bottom line: OCBC is the best-positioned Singapore bank fundamentally, but chasing it 5% extended is not the entry. Wait for a pullback toward SGD 21.50–22.00 before reassessing.

U11_SI.signal_rationale: Signal changed from CAUTION to HOLD — UOB has slipped 1.45% below its 50-day average (SGD 37.43), with RSI at 51.2 in neutral territory and no directional conviction. The prior CAUTION was driven by macro NIM compression, which remains intact, but at 28% below the Singapore cluster median PE (10.1× forward) and a 3.85% yield, the technical condition no longer warrants an active caution flag. Analyst consensus expects a 6.7% earnings decline, and revenue is contracting slightly — the R:R of 0.4:1 to the nearest resistance at SGD 37.40 versus invalidation at the 200-day SMA ($35.62) is unfavourable for entry. No action warranted. Upgrades to WATCH on a sustained reclaim of SGD 37.43 with volume.

C6L_SI.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at CAUTION. Singapore Airlines is the most problematic name in the Singapore cluster: analyst consensus expects a 64.3% earnings decline — this must be the lead consideration, and it is incompatible with a constructive signal. Despite an 8.31% FCF yield and 5.26% dividend yield that superficially look attractive, the earnings trajectory is deeply concerning and forward PE of 18.1× sits 29.3% above the Singapore cluster median without justification. Price is 1.15% below the 50-day SMA with RSI at 47.2 (neutral-bearish). The R:R is distorted with invalidation just 0.8% below at the 200-day SMA ($6.55), and tight_invalidation is true — normal intraday moves can trigger the stop. Structural support at SGD 6.45 is 2.3% below. The near-term Hormuz disruption adds cost pressure via fuel and rerouting surcharges. Bottom line: C6L is priced for conditions that no longer exist — a near-65% expected earnings decline is a thesis-level concern, not a cyclical wobble. Avoid until the earnings trajectory stabilises.

risk_reward.invalidation_reason: 50-day SMA -- break below confirms rally failure

Z74_SI.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at WATCH. Singtel is 2.51% above its 50-day average at SGD 4.91 and has been above it for 44 consecutive sessions with the SMA rising — the extended positioning is real but the trend has duration. RSI at 53 is comfortably neutral. Invalidation at SGD 4.91 (50-day SMA, 2.4% below) is the line that separates a healthy trend from a failed breakout; nearest resistance at SGD 5.15 is only 2.4% above, making the R:R 1.0:1 — insufficient for ACCUMULATE even if other gates passed. The 77.9% PE premium to the Singapore cluster (forward PE 24.9× vs. cluster median 14.0×) is elevated relative to the 0.9% revenue growth rate — growth does not justify the multiple. Volume is below average. The dividend yield of 2.63% is the lowest in the Singapore cluster. Bottom line: Singtel has a clean trend but mediocre near-term R:R and a stretched valuation for its growth rate. Worth watching for a defined entry, but don't chase it above SGD 5.15.

NVDA.signal_rationale: Signal changed from ACCUMULATE to CAUTION — NVDA's R:R has deteriorated to 0.9:1 (nearest support $169.81 vs. nearest resistance $184.47), falling well below the 2.0:1 minimum required for ACCUMULATE, and the prior ACCUMULATE signal from April 1 is no longer supported at today's levels. At $177.39, NVDA sits 2.88% below its declining 50-day SMA ($182.64) with RSI at 49.1 in neutral-bearish territory. Invalidation is at $169.81 (4.3% below, observed support); structural support at $164.30 is 7.4% below. Despite spectacular fundamentals — forward PE 16× with 73.2% revenue growth and PEG 0.72 (well below 1.0, growth significantly outpaces the multiple), plus the Blackwell Ultra ramp confirmed in NVDA's Q4 FY2026 earnings call with Q1 FY2027 guidance of $78B — the near-term technical setup does not support entry. The TurboQuant narrative, now 10 days old, continues to weigh on conviction even if the fundamental thesis remains compelling. FCF yield at 1.35% is thin, reflecting the heavy reinvestment cycle. Per the Benzinga/Counterpoint Research report, hyperscalers are shifting toward ARM-based designs away from x86 — a headwind relevant to NVDA's broader competitive positioning. Bottom line: NVDA is arguably the most fundamentally attractive name on the watchlist at 16× forward earnings for 73% revenue growth, but the technical setup has deteriorated since the ACCUMULATE was triggered. Wait for a price reclaim above $182.64 (50-day SMA) with volume, or a flush to $169–170 support for a better-defined entry.

risk_reward.invalidation_reason: 50-day SMA -- break below confirms rally failure

AMD.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at WATCH. AMD surged 3.37% today to $217.50, reclaiming its 50-day SMA ($211.64) — now 2.77% below — just one session ago. The R:R is distorted (rr_distorted=true): nearest resistance at $219.47 is only 0.9% above, while invalidation at the 50-day SMA ($211.64) is 2.7% below, creating a 0.3:1 ratio that mathematically blocks ACCUMULATE. Structural support at $196.89 (200-day SMA, 9.5% below) is the real downside anchor. The Meta $100B+ GPU supply deal (AMD IR press release, Feb 24, 2026) is the thesis anchor — a multi-year, multi-generation partnership that validates AMD as a credible NVDA alternative at hyperscale. However, per Benzinga/Counterpoint Research today, hyperscalers are structurally shifting toward ARM-based CPU designs away from x86, which is a specific headwind to AMD's legacy CPU franchise even as the GPU business accelerates. AMD earnings are due May 6 (30 days). Trading at 20.2× forward (in line with the semis cluster median) with PEG 0.65 and 34.1% revenue growth — fundamentally compelling. Bottom line: AMD is the cheapest quality name in semis relative to its growth rate, but price has run 6.7% in five days and is pressing the nearest resistance. Wait for a pullback toward $211–213 (50-day SMA) for a cleaner entry, or a clean break above $219.47 on volume.

INTC.signal_rationale: Signal changed from WATCH to CAUTION — Intel has surged 9.1% above its 50-day SMA ($46.16) in just two sessions above, triggering a hard entry block. The 5-day return of 14.24% is extreme for a turnaround story, and with RSI at 60.6 and the 50-day SMA still declining, the pipeline's hard block applies. Forward PE of 50.8× versus the semis cluster median of 20.2× (151.5% premium) is unjustified given negative 4.1% revenue growth, negative FCF yield of -1.78%, and no consensus earnings growth estimate. Per RCR Wireless, Intel's CTO confirmed '18A is the foundation for our future' — the foundry thesis is real, but the stock has priced in significant execution ahead of delivery. The ARM shift reported by Benzinga is a structural headwind to Intel's x86 CPU franchise, which remains the primary revenue driver. Per Benzinga/Counterpoint Research today, hyperscalers are explicitly moving away from Intel's x86 architecture. Intel earnings due April 24 (18 days). Bottom line: Intel's CHIPS Act foundry thesis is intact but at 50.8× forward with negative revenue growth and a hard-blocked entry, this is not the entry point. Revisit on a pullback to the $42–44 zone or after Q1 earnings clarity.

ASML.signal_rationale: Signal changed from HOLD to CAUTION — ASML dropped 3.55% today to $1,317.23 and is now 5.54% below its 50-day SMA ($1,394.45), crossing from HOLD territory into CAUTION. ASML earnings are 9 days away (April 15), creating binary event risk for any entry. The R:R is 2.7:1 (invalidation at $1,283.60 support, 2.6% below; upside target $1,410.41, 7.1% above) with observed quality — mechanically this approaches ACCUMULATE territory, but earnings proximity within 7 days blocks any entry signal. ACCUMULATE gate check: caution_source is 'base_scorer' and ratio is 2.7:1 with rr_quality 'observed', but the earnings-within-5-trading-days rule eliminates ACCUMULATE eligibility for this ticker. Per Simply Wall St., ASML announced 1,700 job cuts with over 1,000 employees reportedly walking out at Veldhoven headquarters — a workforce tension signal that could affect Q2 guidance. Structural support at $1,050.10 (200-day SMA, 20.3% below) is the catastrophic downside if the earnings miss. PEG of 2.18 at 30.1× forward PE is elevated for 4.9% revenue growth and 7.2% expected earnings growth. Bottom line: ASML is the irreplaceable EUV monopoly, but with earnings 9 days out, a workforce walkout in the news, and 5.5% below a declining 50-day average, this is a watch-and-wait. Revisit after April 15 earnings — a beat with positive H2 guidance could flip this to WATCH quickly.

MU.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at CAUTION. Micron sits 9.2% below its 50-day SMA ($403.35) at $366.24 with RSI at 44.4 (neutral-bearish). The R:R is distorted (rr_distorted=true): tight_invalidation is true with the nearest support at $363.75 just 0.7% below — use $357.52 (2.4% below, structural support) as the mental stop, as normal intraday volatility will trigger the $363.75 level. Tight stop — normal intraday volatility may trigger invalidation before the thesis is tested. The valuation picture is anomalous: forward PE of 3.7× with a reported PEG of 0.21 — this PEG appears anomalous given the forward PE; verify the earnings growth estimate before citing as a primary argument. Revenue growth of 196.3% (confirmed via MU's Q2 FY2026 earnings release in March) is real, but the distorted R:R and 9.2% SMA gap block accumulation. Per Motley Fool's April 5 piece, Micron remains 'a top-performing AI semiconductor stock' post its record revenue quarter. The 200-day SMA at $242.91 is 33.7% below — significant air gap. Bottom line: MU is deeply discounted versus its growth but the technical setup is messy with a distorted stop. Wait for stabilisation above $380–390 (approaching the 50-day SMA) before this becomes actionable.

AMZN.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at CAUTION. Amazon is 2.43% below its declining 50-day SMA ($215.00) with RSI at 49.6 — borderline neutral but caution_source is 'base_scorer' reflecting a deteriorating technical trend. ACCUMULATE gate check: ratio is 0.8:1 (invalidation $196.93, upside $219.43) — fails the 2.0:1 minimum. The fundamental picture is mixed: Amazon's $200B AI capex announcement (per AMZN Q4 2025 earnings call) sent shares down 10% on missed Q1 operating income, and analyst consensus expects only 5% earnings growth despite 13.6% revenue growth. FCF yield at 1.06% is thin — the thesis depends heavily on AWS margin expansion, not current cash generation. Per Motley Fool (April 5), Amazon is pursuing Alexa monetisation as the 'next big opportunity.' The 200-day SMA at $224.55 is above price, making it resistance rather than support — a notably weak technical structure. Structural support at $165.29 is 21.2% below. Bottom line: Amazon is caught between its capex investment cycle and near-term earnings pressure. Not actionable until price reclaims $215 (50-day SMA) with improving earnings visibility.

GOOG.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at CAUTION. Alphabet sits 5.02% below its declining 50-day SMA ($310.04) at $294.46, with the 200-day SMA at $265.16 (10.0% below) as the next meaningful structural support. RSI at 47.1 is neutral-bearish. The R:R is unfavourable at 0.6:1 (invalidation at $265.16; upside $311.07). Google earnings are April 24 (18 days), creating event risk. The TurboQuant narrative (now day 10) remains a structural overhang — per Motley Fool (April 5), TurboQuant's announcement triggered persistent memory stock weakness, though Alphabet itself is the disruptor in that dynamic. Per Simply Wall St., Google's AI Overviews have been linked to consumer fraud risks with scammers inserting fake phone numbers — Alphabet is reportedly accelerating anti-spam updates. PEG of 2.21 at 21.9× forward PE is expensive for 18% revenue growth; FCF yield at 1.07% is thin. Analyst consensus expects 31.1% earnings growth — the only fundamental bright spot. Bottom line: GOOG has genuine AI optionality as both disruptor (TurboQuant, TPU v7) and potential victim of its own disruption, but the technical setup is weak and the PEG is stretched. No entry until $310 50-day SMA reclaim.

LITE.sma50_warning: SMA50 is 26% below price — pullback to SMA50 would require a crash-level move. Use shorter-term support levels for entry guidance instead.
LITE.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at CAUTION. Lumentum surged another 8.0% today to $826.88 — now 35.8% above its 50-day SMA, triggering the extreme extension hard block (>20% threshold, no exceptions). The stock is up 108% YTD per Zacks, driven by NVIDIA's confirmed $2B strategic investment (NVIDIA newsroom press release, March 2, 2026) and S&P 500 inclusion on March 9. PEG of 0.17 is anomalous — with a forward PE of 55.2× and negative FCF yield (-0.04%), verify the earnings growth estimate before interpreting this as cheap. The revenue growth of 65.5% is real and impressive, but at 238.3× trailing PE and 22.7% premium to the Photonics cluster median, this is priced for perfection. The 50-day SMA is 26% below price — a pullback to the SMA would require a crash-level move, making shorter-term support at $573.73 (30.6% below) the only relevant entry reference. Bottom line: The NVDA partnership is a genuine structural catalyst, but at 35.8% above its 50-day average the entry risk is extreme. This is a story to watch from the sidelines until a 15–20% pullback creates a defined risk level.

XLE.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at CAUTION. XLE is 7.09% above its 50-day SMA ($55.33) and RSI at 56.3 is above the 55 threshold — hard block on entry applies. Per Bloomberg's Sunday report, Brent climbed 1.4% above $110/bbl on Trump's escalation threats, and per a Reuters-cited JPMorgan note, WTI could spike above $150 if the Hormuz closure extends into mid-May. XLE has been above its 50-day SMA for 66 consecutive sessions with the SMA rising — a structural energy trend — but the RSI condition blocks the trend exception. The R:R of 0.8:1 (derived quality) to resistance at $62.21 vs. invalidation at $55.33 is unattractive at current levels. No entry exists despite the compelling commodity setup. Structural support at $46.50 (200-day SMA, 21.5% below) highlights how far XLE could fall if the Hormuz situation resolves. Bottom line: The energy trade is real but the entry window has closed for now. Becomes interesting on a pullback to $56–57 (near the 50-day SMA), which would require a meaningful de-escalation or oil price correction.

PLTR.entry_block: CAUTION: forward P/E of 80x (>40x). Requires explicit justification of why earnings growth supports this multiple — default to WATCH unless thesis is clearly articulated with specific catalysts and timeline.

PLTR.signal_rationale: Signal changed from CAUTION to WATCH — Palantir has reclaimed its 50-day SMA ($146.91) just one session ago, closing at $148.46 with RSI at 49.5 and a tight 1.06% extension above the SMA. The R:R is 9.1:1 (invalidation $146.91, 1.0% below; upside $161.92, 9.1% above) — however, tight_invalidation is true with invalidation just 1.0% below. Tight stop — normal intraday volatility may trigger invalidation before the thesis is tested. Use $119.17 (structural support, 19.7% below) as the mental stop for position sizing. The pipeline's soft block for forward PE of 79.8× applies — this requires explicit justification. The defense AI thesis is specific and tangible: per the DoD press release, the $448M Navy ShipOS deal is confirmed firm revenue; the $10B Army framework is an IDIQ ceiling (indefinite delivery/indefinite quantity — not firm revenue), not guaranteed spend. Per Rosenblatt analyst notes, price target raised to $200; Daiwa upgraded to Buy with $180 PT. Per TheStreet (April 6), lawmakers sent a stern warning to Palantir — a developing political risk. 2026 revenue guidance of $7.18–7.20B (+61% YoY) per company guidance is the earnings anchor justifying the premium multiple. Volume is below average. Bottom line: PLTR is the purest play on escalating defense AI spending, and the Iran war makes the thesis more urgent, not less. The soft block on valuation is real — 79.8× forward PE — but 61% revenue growth and government contract momentum justify the watch. Entry on a confirmed volume break above $161.92 resistance.

VUAA_L.signal_rationale: Signal changed from WATCH to CAUTION — VUAA.L has slipped further below its 50-day SMA ($130.55), now 3.01% below at $126.62, and RSI at 44.9 is neutral-bearish rather than the oversold extreme seen in late March. The prior WATCH signal reflected proximity to a support test; now the caution_source is 'base_scorer' reflecting ongoing deterioration. ACCUMULATE gate check: caution_source is 'base_scorer', R:R is 2.9:1 (invalidation $125.41, 1.0% below; upside $130.24, 2.9% above), rr_quality is 'observed', rr_distorted is false, VIX is 23.87 (below 30). However, tight_invalidation is true — invalidation at $125.41 is just 1.0% below. Tight stop — normal intraday volatility may trigger invalidation before the thesis is tested. Use $118.86 (structural support, 6.1% below) as the mental stop for position sizing. The 200-day SMA at $127.60 is just above price and acting as resistance. As an ETF (S&P 500 tracker), no PEG, FCF yield, or cluster-relative checks apply — this is purely technical. The macro backdrop (Iran war escalation on Sunday, Fed higher-for-longer, TurboQuant semis overhang) argues for patience. Bottom line: VUAA.L is testing its 200-day average from below with the macro environment still uncertain. Becomes a more attractive entry if it reclaims $127.60 (200-day SMA) with VIX settling below 22.

singapore.summary: The Singapore cluster is in a bifurcated state: DBS (WATCH) and OCBC (extended, CAUTION) are the relative outperformers while UOB (HOLD) and SingAir (CAUTION) drag. NIM compression persists as US10Y at 4.31% stays well above the 4.20% reset threshold. The MAS Monetary Policy Statement on April 14 is the key near-term catalyst that could shift the NIM narrative.
singapore.key_development: OCBC's 5.1% extension above SMA with no entry gate creates a frustrating dynamic — the best-valued bank in the cluster is the least accessible technically.

semiconductors.summary: SOXX at $339.61 (+0.34%) is recovering but remains 18.5% below its pre-TurboQuant peak — the cluster is technically split between NVDA/MU (CAUTION below declining SMAs) and AMD (WATCH, reclaimed SMA50). Per Benzinga/Counterpoint Research today, hyperscalers' structural shift toward ARM-based designs is a new headwind layered atop the TurboQuant narrative, with Intel explicitly named as a loser. ASML's 3.55% drop with earnings 9 days out creates binary risk.
semiconductors.key_development: ARM architecture displacement thesis (Benzinga, April 6) adds a specific revenue headwind to Intel and AMD's CPU franchises that the TurboQuant narrative had not fully priced.

big_tech.summary: AMZN and GOOG remain CAUTION with both below declining 50-day SMAs, while PLTR upgraded to WATCH on its SMA50 reclaim. The cluster is diverging: defense AI (PLTR) is the only constructive setup, while consumer/cloud names face the dual headwind of Fed hawkishness and capex ROI questions.
big_tech.thesis_status: Mixed — PLTR diverging positive, legacy names stuck
big_tech.key_development: PLTR's SMA50 reclaim at $148.46 is the first constructive technical development in the BigTech cluster in two weeks.

photonics.summary: LITE and COHR continue to diverge from the broader semis weakness, with LITE up 8% today (+108% YTD) and COHR up 4.3%. Both are hard-blocked on extension (LITE at 35.8%, COHR at 7.4% above their respective SMAs) — the NVDA $2B investment catalysts are confirmed structural drivers but both names are untouchable at current levels.
photonics.thesis_status: Structurally strong, technically extended — no entry
photonics.key_development: LITE's continued surge to $826.88 despite broad market caution validates the photonics structural theme but makes the risk/reward progressively worse for new entrants.

energy.summary: XLE at $59.25 is hard-blocked 7.1% above its 50-day SMA with the Hormuz crisis entering week 5. Bloomberg's Sunday report of Trump escalation threats and Brent above $110/bbl confirms the geopolitical premium is structural, but the entry window has closed for now.
energy.key_development: Per JPMorgan (cited in Reuters), WTI could spike above $150 if Hormuz remains disrupted into mid-May — the scenario is severe enough to watch the $56–57 pullback zone.

index.summary: VUAA.L slipped to CAUTION as it trades 3% below a declining 50-day SMA with the 200-day acting as resistance — the broad S&P 500 trend is at an inflection. VIX at 23.87 is below the 25 threshold but Sunday's Trump-Iran escalation threatens a gap-down open Monday.
index.key_development: Monday's open is high-risk given Sunday night futures decline on Trump's Iran threats — this is the most immediate event risk on the watchlist.

interconnected.name: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing (TSMC)
interconnected.reason: AMD's Meta supply deal and NVDA's Blackwell Ultra ramp both run through TSMC fabs — high utilisation rates are the direct read-through from both WATCH/CAUTION positions.
interconnected.entry_note: Add to watchlist if TSM (US ADR) pulls back 8–10% from current levels on broad semis weakness or Taiwan geopolitical noise.

interconnected.name: Broadcom
interconnected.reason: Custom AI ASIC demand from hyperscalers (AMZN, GOOG) is a direct Broadcom revenue driver — the capex sustainability debate in BigTech directly affects AVGO's custom chip order book.
interconnected.entry_note: Revisit after GOOG and AMZN Q1 earnings (April 24 and May 1 respectively) to gauge whether hyperscaler custom silicon budgets held firm.

interconnected.name: Arm Holdings
interconnected.reason: Today's Benzinga/Counterpoint Research report specifically names ARM-based CPU designs as the beneficiary of hyperscalers moving away from Intel and AMD x86 — the most direct positive read-through from today's dominant new story.
interconnected.entry_note: Becomes interesting for watchlist inclusion if ARM pulls back 10% from current levels following the broader semis weakness — the structural shift to ARM architecture is a multi-year tailwind.

active_risks: US-Israel-Iran war (Day 28+): Hormuz effectively closed, 12–15M bpd disrupted; Trump threatening attacks on Iranian infrastructure over weekend
US-China tech decoupling: ARM shift and COMAC restrictions accelerating; China rare earth export controls as retaliation
Fed higher-for-longer: US10Y at 4.31% constraining tech multiples with April CPI critical for rate-cut probability reset
Google TurboQuant (Day 10): Structural GPU-demand paradigm uncertainty persists; ARM architecture shift adds new dimension today
geopolitical.market_impact: The Hormuz crisis is directly reflected in WTI above $110 (with Sunday escalation pushing Brent above $110) and XLE's 7% SMA extension with a hard-blocked entry — PLTR is the most direct beneficiary of the defense AI spending surge it has generated. The TurboQuant and ARM architecture shifts are jointly suppressing the semis cluster with SOXX still 18.5% below its pre-announcement peak, keeping NVDA, MU, and ASML all in CAUTION despite compelling fundamental valuations.
geopolitical.new_since_yesterday: Trump renewed threats Sunday to attack Iranian infrastructure if Hormuz remains closed (Bloomberg); Brent climbed 1.4% above $110/bbl in Sunday trading with stock futures falling — Monday open expected volatile.
geopolitical.portfolio_action: Hold cash with selective WATCH positions in AMD and PLTR; avoid new entries into extended names (LITE, XLE, OCBC) and maintain caution on Monday's open given Sunday night escalation signals.

events_this_week.event: US equity market open — expected volatility from weekend Trump-Iran escalation and oil surge

portfolio_snapshot.risk_posture: Sunday's Trump escalation of the Iran conflict is the single most immediate risk — a volatile Monday open could push several WATCH signals back to CAUTION if the semis cluster sells off further on ARM displacement fears layered atop the Hormuz premium.
